scope.target: v0.4.0 file-disposition and downstream upgrade contract
constraints: Every remediated framework path receives kept, moved-to, merged-into, or retired disposition
The manifest informs but never bypasses three-way comparison
Retirement and target-owned changes remain reconciliation-safe
Complete before ROUTE-001 and release closure
non_goals: Upgrade either downstream lab in this workflow
Update target provenance before a real target upgrade validates

steps: Maintain exact-path dispositions as remediation tasks change files
Define consumer validation and migration-guide projection
Reconcile moved, merged, and retired paths with upgrader safety rules
Validate manifest completeness before ROUTE-001 and release preparation
validation: Changed-path coverage against the remediation commit range
execution.last_completed_step: Validated every distributable framework path changed since ed5f8fb with Git-backed exact-case dispositions and projected safe move, merge, retirement, and target-owned reconciliation rules into the planned v0.4.0 release.
execution.next_action: Start ROUTE-001 audience placement and inbound-route remediation.

results.summary: Completed the v0.4.0 file-disposition contract, added reusable Git-backed validation, taught ai-context-upgrader to treat dispositions as incoming intent only, and registered planned release notes and migration guidance without creating a tag or claiming publication.
tests_run: File-disposition manifest validation passed with complete Git changed-path coverage
AI context version validation passed for 5 release records
AI context and shell asset validators passed
results.residual_risk: REL-v0.4.0 remains planned. Package build/parity, independent verification, final commit identity, and user-created annotated tag are later release gates.
